text.title: Ad Set Creation, Targeting, and Bid Strategies
text.summary: Ad set structure with geo/demographic targeting, bid strategy options, optimization goals, and Facebook placement configuration.

list.item: LOWEST_COST_WITHOUT_CAP: maximize results within budget — use for learning phase
COST_CAP: control cost per result — use when CAC target is known
LOWEST_COST_WITH_BID_CAP: maximum bid per auction — use for strict cost control
LOWEST_COST_WITH_MIN_ROAS: minimum return on ad spend — use for e-commerce with known ROAS target
OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS: website sales or leads — requires Pixel with conversion events
LANDING_PAGE_VIEWS: quality traffic — filters bot clicks

rule.statement: Match optimization_goal to billing_event: OFFSITE_CONVERSIONS uses IMPRESSIONS billing; LINK_CLICKS uses IMPRESSIONS billing. Mismatched pairs cause ad set rejection.
rule.rationale: Meta validates goal/billing combinations server-side. Rejected ad sets fail silently unless you check the API error response.

section.title: Placement Options
section.p: Facebook positions: feed, right_hand_column, instant_article, marketplace, video_feeds, story, search, instream_video, facebook_reels. Specify publisher_platforms as ["facebook"] to limit to Facebook only.

antipattern.description: Using Advantage+ Placements (automatic) when creative is only sized for feed (1:1 ratio).
antipattern.reason: Automatic placements will show the ad in Stories (9:16) and Reels where a 1:1 creative is cropped, degrading performance and brand appearance.

text.reference: cURL example for creating an ad set with full targeting configuration
